user_stories.goal: I want to enter a destination, trip length, budget level, and interests so that the system generates a personalised day-by-day travel itinerary.
user_stories.benefit: So I get a ready-to-follow plan tailored to my constraints and preferences without manual research.
acceptance_criteria: A web form and API accept destination, trip_length (positive integer), budget_level (e.g., low/medium/high), and interests (list of strings).
Inputs are validated and user receives clear validation messages for missing/invalid fields.

The returned itinerary contains a 'days' array with length equal to trip_length and each day contains at least: list of activities, suggested times, estimated durations, and per-activity cost estimates.
The itinerary recommendations reflect declared interests (majority of activities match interests) and budget_level (estimated total cost falls within the defined range for that level).

user_stories.goal: I want to view the generated itinerary on the website in a clear day-by-day layout so I can follow it during my trip.
user_stories.benefit: So I can easily understand what to do each day, when, and how to get there.
acceptance_criteria: The website displays the itinerary with separate sections for each day labeled by day number or date.
Each activity entry shows name, short description, suggested start time or time window, estimated duration, and a transport suggestion or travel time to next activity.
There is a printable view or export (e.g., print-friendly HTML or PDF) accessible from the UI that formats the day-by-day itinerary for offline use.
Links to maps or directions are provided for activities where applicable.

user_stories.goal: I want a Flask API endpoint that accepts itinerary requests and returns structured JSON so other services and the website can integrate with the itinerary generator.
user_stories.benefit: So the itinerary functionality can be reused programmatically and the website can use the same backend logic.
acceptance_criteria: A POST /api/itinerary endpoint accepts JSON with keys: destination, trip_length, budget_level, interests and returns HTTP 200 with a JSON payload containing a days array and activity details.
The API returns appropriate HTTP error codes and JSON error messages for invalid input (e.g., 400 for missing/invalid fields).
Responses are deterministic for the same inputs (or document when randomness is applied) and include metadata such as estimated total cost and generation timestamp.
CORS is configured so the website frontend can call the API in a browser.

user_stories.goal: I want to refine or regenerate my itinerary by adjusting inputs (e.g., change interests or trip length) and compare versions so I can choose the best plan.
user_stories.benefit: So I can iteratively improve the itinerary until it fits my needs.
acceptance_criteria: The UI provides an 'Edit' or 'Refine' action that pre-fills the original inputs and allows changes.
Submitting refinements calls the API and replaces the displayed itinerary with the new one while preserving access to the previous version in the session.
The user can view at least the current and previous itinerary versions and can revert to a previous version within the same session.
